text: Continuous Integration (CI) is a software development practice where developers regularly merge their code changes into a shared main repository. The key to CI is automated builds, which compile and test the code every time a change is pushed. After merging, the CI pipeline automatically runs a series of tests to check for errors or bugs. This process helps in catching issues early, before they become bigger problems later in development. By running tests frequently, teams can fix problems quickly and keep the codebase stable. CI pipelines make this whole process smooth and efficient, allowing developers to work faster and with more confidence.
